### Post by tech_monkee on 2007-04-11
fresh ubuntu install.

ntfs-config caught both my hdds.
it loaded and mounted one fine but not the other. also it did not 'see' my external ntfs hdd.

here's my fstab.
```
# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/mapper/sda1 :
UUID=0c667681-1579-45b6-a7dc-3862d28df5bf / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/mapper/sda5 :
UUID=33e520e5-b2c0-4034-b46f-44c6aa87a85a none swap sw 0 0
/dev/cdrom /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
/dev/sdb1 /media/capture ntfs umask=222,utf8 0 0
```

can anyone shed some light on what that error is and how to correct. mind you i can not boot into windows anymore since i wiped my main drive and don't (can't find) my ghosted cd :(
